current_task: PPO running v143_sc (iter 23/30). Two bugs fixed this session: (1) panel_eval combined_wr formula was measuring total USSR/US wins not our model's wins (always ~0.5 noise), now fixed to use index-based counting from single benchmark_model_vs_model_batched call; (2) fixture pool was all old-engine models 100-175 ELO above current model (1944), now updated to include v138-v142_sc (ELO 1944-1979) for near-equal training signal.
next_tasks: Monitor v144_sc ELO with fixed panel eval + balanced fixtures — expect ELO stabilization or improvement
If ELO still declining after v145_sc: consider LR increase (2e-5 → 5e-5) or games_per_iter increase (200→400)
GAP-005: Core ops placement legality (complex, lower priority)
Python engine full removal: migrate 51 callers to tscore bindings, then delete python/tsrl/engine/
Task #46: Hash-based match cache identity (key by file SHA256, not name)
active_background: v143_sc PPO training (auto-chains to v144_sc with balanced fixtures)
completed_this_session: Panel eval formula bug fixed: combined_wr was counting total USSR/US wins (always ~0.5 noise), now uses index-based counting from single call so it measures OUR model's actual WR
Fixture pool rebalanced: added v138_sc(1979), v140_sc(1949), v142_sc(1944), v67_sc(2017) to both USSR and US pools; both pools now symmetric (same 8 opponents) to prevent USSR/US training bias
Previous session: Probe parquet mode_mask fix, v128_sc restart, ELO regression diagnosis, v132_sc reset from v77_sc (ELO 2119), W&B chart improvements

root_cause_diagnosis.panel_eval_bug: benchmark_model_vs_model_batched returns results ordered [model_a=USSR games, model_a=US games]. Old code counted ALL USSR wins from r_a (including opponent USSR wins in second half) + ALL US wins from r_b (including opponent US wins). Expected value always 0.5 regardless of opponent strength. Fixed: single call, count only our model's wins by result index.
root_cause_diagnosis.fixture_asymmetry: All previous fixtures (v48,v55,v45,v19-v22) were old-engine models at ELO 2042-2118, while our current model is at 1944. Model consistently loses 70-75% of games → tiny positive advantage signal → PPO can't learn effectively. Near-equal opponents (v138-v142_sc at 1944-1979) give WR near 50% → maximum PFSP weight → best learning signal.
root_cause_diagnosis.elo_decline: v132_sc started at 2092 and declined to 1944 over 10 runs primarily due to: (1) inaccurate panel eval HWM selection (noise-based), (2) fixture pool too hard causing one-sided loss data, (3) model drifting from USSR-strong to US-strong due to fixture asymmetry

context: v143_sc iter20 panel eval (first with correct formula) showed avg=0.552 vs [v55,v54,v44,v45,v14] — plausible for a model at ~2092 ELO (v77_sc-derived). The panel eval HWM is now set to iter20. v144_sc will use the balanced fixture pool and correctly-computed panel eval. If learning is still stalled, next lever is LR increase or games_per_iter increase.
